violations.text: Beer cheese, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Beer cheese observed in the prep unit with a temperature of 42 degrees F. Proper cooling methods reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Beer cheese was placed hot in the prep unit in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

The doors to the outside, located at the side of the bar, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the bar dishwasher and the 3-bay sink. Test strips must be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026
The doors to the restrooms are not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

Doors to the outside, located at the entrance and receiving, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2024
The doors to the restrooms are not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2024
